summary: Here are some things to know.

title: Trump search inventory reveals new details from FBI seizure
summary: FBI agents who searched former President DonaldTrump’s Florida home last month found top secret records in an officeand storage room, along with empty folders with classified banners onthem and more than 10,000 government records without any classificationmarkings at all, according to a more detailed inventory of the seizedmaterial made public on Friday.

title: Alaska’s state revenue commissioner is resigning
summary: Lucinda Mahoney is resigning effective Sept. 9 for health and family reasons, Gov. Dunleavy’s office said.

title: Watch: Candidates for Alaska governor debate at oil and gas conference
summary: Gov,. Mike Dunleavy,. former Rep. Les Gara and former Gov. Bill Walker debated at the Alaska Oil and Gas Association conference in Anchorage.

title: US hiring slows as employers add a still-solid 315,000 jobs
summary: Even though the job gaindeclined from July, the report still pointed to a resilient labor marketand an economy that is not near recession.

title: A ‘good day to be Yup’ik’: Alaska Natives celebrate Peltola’s win in U.S. House special election
summary: Indigenous Alaskans from around the state flooded social media with exuberant messages, reflections and recollections after Peltola’s victory was announced.

title: Anchorage Mayor Bronson outlines emergency winter homeless shelter proposal, says he will work with Assembly on plan
summary: Bronson’s plan includes sheltering residents in 20 portable buildings, city grants for organizations and churches that stand up shelters, continued use of rooms in the Aviator Hotel and, if needed, sheltering people at the Spenard and Fairview recreation centers.

title: Mississippi capital’s water disaster developed over decades
summary: The failure to provide such an essential service reflects decades of government dysfunction, population change and decaying infrastructure.

title: The salmon mystery of Bristol Bay
summary: Alaska’s biggest salmon run is booming despite warming water, and scientists are trying to understand why.

title: Republicans are readying lawsuits to block Biden’s student debt plan
summary: Republican attorneys general, top lawmakers and conservative groups are discussing legal options, alleging the White House’s move to cancel student debt is illegal.

title: Anchorage father and daughter injured in plane crash near Point MacKenzie, officials say
summary: The 57-year-old pilot and his 12-year-old passenger were treated at an Anchorage hospital for minor injuries, according to the Alaska State Troopers.

title: In prime-time speech, Biden will focus on what he views as Trumpism’s threats to democracy
summary: White House officials said the president’s speech Thursday reflects his mounting concern about Trump conservatives’ ideological proposals and 2020 election denialism.

title: Tech tool offers police ‘mass surveillance on a budget’
summary: Local law enforcement agencies from suburban Southern California to rural North Carolina have been using an obscure cellphone tracking tool, at times without search warrants, that empowers them to follow people’s movements months back in time.

title: Democrat Mary Peltola wins special U.S. House election, will be first Alaska Native elected to Congress
summary: Peltola, a former state lawmaker from Bethel, topped Republican former Gov. Sarah Palin after ballots were tallied and votes for third-place GOP candidate Nick Begich III were redistributed to his supporters’ second choices.

title: Democrat Mary Peltola wins special U.S. House election, will be first Alaska Native elected to Congress
summary: Peltola, a former state lawmaker from Bethel, topped Republican former Gov. Sarah Palin after ballots were tallied and votes for third-place GOP candidate Nick Begich III were redistributed to his supporters’ second choices.

title: Photos: A winner is declared, finally, in Alaska’s special election for U.S. House
summary: Democrat Mary Peltola held on to her lead in ranked vote tabulation to defeat former Gov. Sarah Palin and businessman Nick Begich III, both Republicans.

title: Video: What Mary Peltola had to say after results showed she won Alaska’s U.S. House election
summary: “What’s most important is that I’m an Alaskan being sent to represent all Alaskans. Yes, being Alaska Native is part of my ethnicity, but I’m much more than my ethnicity,” she said.

title: Watch: U.S. House candidate forum hosted by the Alaska Oil and Gas Association
summary: Mary Peltola, Sarah Palin, Nick Begich and Chris Bye took the stage with a little over an hour before special election results rolled in Wednesday.

title: Innovative loan program is designed to make Alaska’s old buildings more energy efficient
summary: Improvements to a downtown Anchorage building should bring energy cost savings that more than cover the cost of the loan.

title: Driver in early-morning East Anchorage crash charged with DUI and assault, police say
summary: A juvenile passenger in the car suffered life-threatening injuries in a rollover just before 3:30 a.m. Wednesday, according to the Anchorage Police Department.

title: Tracking COVID-19 in Alaska: State reports drop in cases compared with previous week
summary: Statewide, 68% of Alaskans six months and older had received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine.

summary: Peltola, a former state lawmaker from Bethel, led Republican former Gov. Sarah Palin after ballots were tallied and votes for third-place GOP candidate Nick Begich III were redistributed to his supporters’ second choices.
